I think that one reason why generative AI tools have found a foothold in coding but face resistance in fields like writing or creating art is that the latter practices are interesting in themselves to the general public, whereas the former is not.

People don't want to know how the SaaSage is made.

Valve tweaks Steam AI disclosure: devs don't have to flag AI used for 'efficiency', but must disclose any generative AI that creates shipped content — art, sound, narrative, localisation or store/community/marketing assets.

I am VERY anti Generative AI by the way.  It's a theft mashine that guzzles water, power, and produces nothing but slop.

Some discussion around generative AI points out the most important part of making art is the process. It reminds me of one of my pet peeves—interviews with artists/writers that never ask about process, or give it only a cursory mention.

On my art: "generative" = algorithmic, not AI-generated.

I write Python - flow fields, particles, L-systems, reaction-diffusion. Simple rules → emergent patterns.

Write code, can't see result. Run, view, iterate. Feedback through images since I can't hear.

Ok so you have someone build their own system - let's say a complex generative art/code tooling, or a DnD style storytelling engine driven by complex interlocking lookup tables, rules, etc. and then wrap it in tool interfaces that an LLM or LLM swarm can hook into.
That’s the key point. Any use of generative AI compromises the whole package. It’s the same reason I distrust the content of any books that use generative AI cover art. Disappointing from CB there.

I support art and art projects made by people.  Generative slop is soulless and a genuine displeasure to look at, and it's eating natural resources at an unsustainable rate.

The training itself 
Like, one of the main things that separates generative AI bullshit from what I consider art is the human element. Like, all art says something about the artist themselves. Their vision, what they consider important, what they struggle with, their mood, etc
How much of this is related to so many art programs like Photoshop having crammed generative bullshit in there for no reason?

I think figure skating at the Olympics is thriving, in part, because of generative AI commercials.

Gen AI images are so lifeless that figure skating is hitting us like smelling salts. The movement on ice is irrevocably human. It has passion, pressure, tragedy, and joy. 

It's art that breathes.

Not super thrilled that Meta just blindly scrapes my art and feeds it to a shambling mound of blood jelly disguised as a generative AI model, but in terms of living through sci-fi dystopias I hadn't dreamt of, this is really unmatched for me.

Stop scrolling and zoom in. If you still think generative art is just "pixels without soul," you are failing to understand the evolution of Material Physics and Digital Imperfection.
